Text: What is the difference between an abstract class and an interface in Java?
Options: Abstract classes support multiple inheritance while interfaces do not;Abstract classes can have state and implemented methods while interfaces define a pure contract (Java 8+ allows default methods);Interfaces cannot be extended;Abstract classes cannot have constructors

Text: What does the 'final' keyword mean when applied to a class in Java?
Options: The class must implement an interface;The class cannot be subclassed;The class cannot have any methods;The class runs faster
Redefining a method in a subclass;Defining multiple methods with the same name but different parameter signatures in the same class;Calling a method recursively;Overriding a final method

Text: What is the Java memory model concept of the heap vs the stack?
Options: Heap is faster than stack;Stack stores local variables and call frames while the heap stores objects allocated with new and is managed by the garbage collector

Text: What is the Singleton design pattern?
Options: A pattern to create multiple instances;A creational pattern that restricts a class to a single instance and provides a global access point
An object can only belong to one type;A single interface or method can represent different underlying forms allowing objects to be treated as instances of their parent type

Text: What is the difference between '==' and '.equals()' in Java?
Options: They are identical for all types;'==' compares object references while '.equals()' compares the logical equality of object content

Text: What does the Java keyword 'volatile' do?
Options: Makes a variable constant;Ensures that reads and writes to a variable are visible to all threads by reading and writing directly to main memory

Text: What is the purpose of the Java 'try-with-resources' statement?
Options: To retry a failed block;To automatically close resources that implement AutoCloseable when the try block exits

Text: What is the difference between ArrayList and LinkedList in Java?
Options: They are internally identical;ArrayList uses a dynamic array (fast random access) while LinkedList uses a doubly-linked list (fast insert/delete at ends)

Text: What is the Builder design pattern used for?
Options: Building network connections;Constructing complex objects step-by-step separating construction logic from the final object

Text: What does the @Override annotation do in Java?
Options: Forces method overloading;Indicates that the method overrides a superclass method and triggers a compile-time error if it does not

Text: Write a Java class called 'Stack<T>' that implements a generic stack using an ArrayList with push(T item) pop() peek() and isEmpty() methods.

Write a Java implementation of the Observer design pattern with a Subject class that maintains a list of Observer interfaces and notifies them on update. Include a ConcreteObserver that prints the update.

Write a Java function called 'mergeIntervals' that takes a List of int[] intervals where each interval is [start end] and returns a merged list with overlapping intervals combined. Sort by start time first.
